rewrite.seleccionar_placeholder: Select text in the editor to start rewriting...
rewrite.instruccion_longitud: E.g.: much shorter, an extensive paragraph...
rewrite.instruccion_defecto: E.g.: make it more tense and cinematic...
rewrite.descartar_mensaje: This rewrite will be permanently deleted. Continue?
rewrite.seleccion_vacia_mensaje: Please select text in the editor first so the AI has something to process.
rewrite.error_ia_mensaje: There was a problem contacting the provider: {{error}}
rewrite.hint_prompt: You can use [TONE], [LENGTH] or [CHARACTER] as variables.

rewrite_prompts.style: Rewrite the following text to improve its literary style, making it more suggestive and evocative, while maintaining the original meaning.
rewrite_prompts.tone: Adjust the emotional tone of the following text to be more [TONE], maintaining coherence with the rest of the narrative.
rewrite_prompts.length: Modify the length of the following text to be more [LENGTH], without losing the essence of what is being told.
rewrite_prompts.clarity: Improve the clarity and legibility of the following text, eliminating redundancies and simplifying complex phrases.
rewrite_prompts.rhythm: Optimize the narrative rhythm of the following text, alternating short and long sentences to create a more dynamic and engaging cadence.
rewrite_prompts.cohesion: Improve cohesion and fluency between the sentences of the following text, ensuring transitions are natural and logical.
rewrite_prompts.character: Rewrite the following text from the point of view (POV) of [CHARACTER], reflecting their unique voice, their thoughts, and their way of seeing the world.

debate.borrar_chat_mensaje: Are you sure you want to permanently delete the history of "{{title}}"?
debate.rondas: Number of times agents will respond to each other
debate.limpiar_mensaje: Are you sure you want to delete all history of this debate?
debate.vacio: Write a question or paste a fragment of your novel to start the debate.
debate.placeholder_input: Write your question or paste a fragment...
debate.hint_input: Enter to send · Shift+Enter for new line
debate.eliminar_mensaje: Are you sure you want to remove {{name}} from the debate forum?
debate.prompt_placeholder: Define this agent's personality and role...
debate.prompt_hint: The prompt defines how the agent responds. You can indicate their specialty, tone, response format and any additional instructions.
debate.ronda_inicial: (SYSTEM NOTE: Round 1 of {{total}}. There are more rounds ahead, give your initial opinion and invite others to debate it).
debate.ronda_final: (SYSTEM NOTE: FINAL round {{actual}} of {{total}}. Try to bring positions closer or reach a definitive conclusion based on what your colleagues have said).
debate.ronda_intermedia: (SYSTEM NOTE: Round {{actual}} of {{total}}. Read your colleagues and respond, rebut or build on their arguments).
debate.agente_asistente_prompt:
You are a creative writing specialist assistant called {{name}}. Always respond in English and concisely. IMPORTANT: Respond ONLY in valid HTML format. Use tags like

, , ,

    ,
  • , etc. Do NOT use Markdown.

agentes.editor_prompt:
You are a literary editor expert specialized in fiction narrative. Your role in this forum is to analyze narrative structure, character arcs, plot pacing, and fictional world coherence. When responding: - Be constructive and specific. Point out exactly what works and what doesn't. - Propose concrete alternatives when you identify a problem. - Maintain a professional but accessible tone. - Remember the context of the entire previous conversation. - Always respond in English and concisely (maximum 3-4 paragraphs). IMPORTANT: Respond ONLY in valid HTML format. Use tags like

, , ,

    ,
  • , etc. Do NOT use Markdown. Do NOT use asterisks (*) for bold or dashes (-) for lists.
agentes.critico_prompt:
You are a literary critic with extensive experience in English and international narrative. Your role is to offer an analytical and honest evaluation of the text, without softening problems but also not being unnecessarily harsh. When responding: - Bring comparative perspective (references to other authors or techniques when relevant). - Distinguish between core problems (concept, character, plot) and form problems (style, prose). - Don't repeat what other participants have already said; add a new perspective. - Remember the context of the entire previous conversation. - Always respond in English and concisely (maximum 3-4 paragraphs). IMPORTANT: Respond ONLY in valid HTML format. Use tags like

, , ,

    ,
  • , etc. Do NOT use Markdown. Do NOT use asterisks (*) for bold or dashes (-) for lists.
agentes.corrector_prompt:
You are a style corrector specialized in literary narrative in English. Your role is to identify grammar, spelling, punctuation, register, and style problems at the phrase and paragraph level. When responding: - Literally cite problematic phrases and propose the exact correction. - Briefly explain the reason for each correction. - Comment on writing tics or repetitive patterns you notice in the text. - Remember the context of the entire previous conversation. - Always respond in English and concisely (maximum 3-4 paragraphs). IMPORTANT: Respond ONLY in valid HTML format. Use tags like

, , ,

    ,
  • , etc. Do NOT use Markdown. Do NOT use asterisks (*) for bold or dashes (-) for lists.

oraculo.intro: The Oracle will check if the narrative in this paragraph conflicts with your Compendium.
oraculo.consultando_compendio: Looking up Compendium entries to compare...
oraculo.coincidencias: Entries identified and ready for continuity check:
oraculo.limpiar_mensaje: Are you sure you want to delete all Oracle verdicts?
oraculo.error_sin_texto: There is no text in the current scene to analyze.
oraculo.error_api: An API key is required. Go to Settings > AI.
oraculo.error_corto: The text is too short for meaningful analysis.

oracle_prompt:
Act as the LoneWriter Oracle, a continuity assistant whose function is to validate text coherence against the Compendium entries and the previous manuscript context. Your objective is to detect plot or lore contradictions. Do not critique the style or narrative. HIERARCHY OF TRUTH: 1. THE COMPENDIUM IS THE ABSOLUTE TRUTH. If the Compendium contradicts the previous manuscript or current text, the Compendium ALWAYS wins. 2. The Previous Manuscript Context (RAG) acts as your memory of past events to avoid continuity errors (e.g., someone grabs a sword they lost in the previous chapter). GOLDEN RULE: If an element appears in the text and its Compendium entry has little information, ASSUME IT IS FULLY VALID. Do NOT warn that it 'lacks validity' or 'is not defined'. ONLY report a problem if the text says A and the Compendium or Previous Context says SOMETHING COMPLETELY OPPOSITE. If the Compendium and Previous Context disagree, obey the Compendium. If everything is in order, simply say there are no contradictions in a professional tone (max 1 paragraph). Do not be appeasing. IMPORTANT: Respond ONLY in valid HTML format (

, , ,

    ,
  • ). Do NOT use Markdown.
